data.narrative:
Intuitive Machines is a space and aerospace company at the SPAC stage that has raised $305M to date. The comparable universe consists of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private competitors including SpaceX and Blue Origin, though the private set includes a wide range of maturity and business models.

Standard valuation methods face material constraints here. Peer-median analysis is inapplicable: while 23 same-sector peers exist, only 2 operate at the same stage with no adjacent-stage or similar-tier comparables, falling short of the minimum 3-datapoint threshold required for defensible triangulation. Public-comp anchoring is similarly blocked by the absence of disclosed revenue figures—private companies rarely report this metric, and the public Space & Aerospace median P/S of 53.2x cannot be applied without a target revenue baseline. The 5 public comps trade at a 53.2x sector median valuation multiple, but this establishes sector context rather than a direct valuation input.

Given these constraints, a quantitative valuation range cannot be reliably derived from the available data. Any range would rest on either insufficient comparable datapoints or imputed revenue assumptions not grounded in disclosed figures. The analysis instead identifies key uncertainties: revenue scale and trajectory remain undisclosed, and the composition of the private comp set (from pre-revenue stages through SpaceX) is too heterogeneous to support meaningful benchmarking. Further disclosure or alternative valuation frameworks anchored to development milestones would be required to support a specific valuation conclusion.
